title: Delegation and Verification Under AI
abstract: arXiv:2603.02961v2 Announce Type: replace-cross  Abstract: As AI systems enter institutional workflows, workers must decide whether to delegate task execution to AI and how much effort to invest in verifying AI outputs, while institutions evaluate workers using outcome-based standards that may misalign with workers' private costs. We model delegation and verification as the solution to a rational worker's optimization problem, and define worker quality by evaluating an institution-centered utility (distinct from the worker's objective) at the resulting optimal action. We formally characterize optimal worker workflows and show that AI induces *phase transitions*, where arbitrarily small differences in verification ability lead to sharply different behaviors. As a result, AI can amplify workers with strong verification reliability while degrading institutional worker quality for others who rationally over-delegate and reduce oversight, even when baseline task success improves and no beha

translated_title: 人工智能下的委托与验证
translated_abstract: 随着人工智能系统进入机构工作流程，工作人员必须决定是否将任务执行委托给人工智能，以及在验证人工智能输出结果上投入多少精力，而机构则使用基于结果的标准来评估工作人员，这些标准可能与工作人员的私人成本不一致。我们将委托与验证建模为理性工作人员优化问题的解，并通过评估以由此产生的最优行动为中心的机构效用（区别于工作人员自身目标）来定义工作人员质量。我们正式刻画了最优工作人员工作流程，并表明人工智能会引发“相变”，即验证能力上任意微小的差异都会导致截然不同的行为。因此，即使基线任务成功率有所提高且没有行为变化，人工智能也可能放大具有强验证可靠性的工作人员的能力，同时降低其他理性过度委托并减少监督的工作人员在机构中的质量。
tldr: 本文建模并证明，在AI辅助工作流程中，验证能力上的微小差异会引发工作人员行为的“相变”，导致AI可能提升强验证者质量，却因理性过度委托而降低其他工作人员在机构中的整体表现。
en_tdlr: This paper models and proves that in AI-assisted workflows, small differences in verification ability cause "phase transitions" in worker behavior, where AI can amplify strong verifiers while degrading institutional quality for others due to rational over-delegation.
